title: begonia subacutata
intro: Begonia subacutata is a species of flowering plant in the family Begoniaceae, known for its distinctive, hairy leaves and small white flowers. Traditionally, this plant is used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Begonia subacutata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or as a poultice, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
